sutta: is quite a good suta once the exalted 1 dwelt among the sakyans in banyan tree at kapilavatu there mahanama the sakyan came and visited him and after saluting sat down at 1 side there addressing the exalted 1 he said lord how becomes a man a lay disciple and the buddha said when mahanama he has found refuge in the buddha found refuge in the dhamma found refuge in the sangha then he is a lay disciple lord how is a lay disciple virtuous when mahanama a lay disciple abstains from taking life abstains from taking what is not given to him abstains from lustful and evil conduct abstains from lying and abstains from spirits and intoxicants the cause of indolence then a lay disciple is virtuous lord how does a lay disciple help on his own welfare but not that of another when mahanama he has achieved faith for himself but strives not to compass faith in another has achieved virtue for self but strives not to compass virtue in another has achieved himself renunciation but strives not to compass renunciation in another longs himself to see the monks but strives not for this side for another longs himself to hear such dharma or true dhamma but strives not for this hearing for another is mindful himself of dhamma he has heard but strives not that another should be mindful of it reflects himself upon the meaning of dhamma he is mindful of but strives not for another to reflect their own but he knows himself both the letter and the spirit of dhamma and walks in conformity there with but strives not for another soul to walk then a lay disciple helps on his own welfare but not that of another and how lord does a lay disciple help on both his own welfare and the welfare of another when indeed mahanama he has achieved faith himself and strives to compose faith in another as as he has achieved virtue himself and strives to compass virtue in another renunciation longs to see the monks to hear saddam his mindful reflex but he knows both the letter and the spirit of dhamma and walks in conformity there with and strives to make another sort of wrong then mahanama a lay disciple helps on his own welfare and the welfare of another it's the end of the sutta
commentary:
this suta is quite important because here it gives a definition of a lay disciple a lay disciple is 1 who has taken refuge in the buddha dhamma and sangha and you just have to take refuge on your own and you are already considered a lay disciple it's only nowadays sometimes there is a ceremony but there don't need to be a ceremony if you on your own in your own heart take refuge to the buddha dharma and the sangha then you are already a lay disciple and a lay disciple is virtuous by keeping the 5 precepts so the 5 precepts are the practice of a lay disciple and then the lady disciples helps himself if he achieves these few things he has faith has virtue practices renunciation he hears the true dhamma saddam which means the discourses the original discourses of the buddha and he is mindful of dhamma he reflects upon the meaning of dharma that he has heard and then knowing both the letter and the spirit of dhamma he walks in conformity there with and he practices all these things then he is helping himself but if he practices these things and tries to help others to practice these things then he not only benefits himself but benefits others that's why in 1 of the sutas the buddha said that those people that you love those who are near and dear to you especially your family members it is good to bring them to have faith in the buddha dharma and sangha that way you will benefit them a lot and also if you hope to meet them in the future life it's also important to bring them to have faith in the buddha dharma and sangha because the buddha said if 2 persons are to meet together in the next life 4 things should be the same 1 is faith another 1 is conduct moral that means equally virtuous or equally evil and the third 1 is charity equally generous or equally selfish and the fourth 1 is wisdom equally wise or equally foolish so if we bring our near and dear relatives and friends to have faith in the buddha dharma and sangha and practice like we practice and then the chances of meeting them together in the next life would be better


===== SOURCE: 074_Anguttara Nikaya Book 8E 8327 - 8435 by Bhante Hye Dhammavuddho Mahathera.txt =====
